_doc: Verbatim, scrubbed responses captured from the original uipath-troubleshoot session that diagnosed a Faulted GSuite Download File job in folder Shared whose configured Integration Service connection could not be resolved/authorized. Job/folder keys, the bogus connection id (00000000-1111-2222-3333-444444444444), and all exception text are preserved exactly; hostname, email, and Windows account were scrubbed (UIP-PW06WJSK -> MOCK-HOST, dan.morosanu@uipath.com -> original_email@test.com, UIPATH\DAN.MOROSANU -> UIPATH\REPLACEMENT_USER). First-match-wins: specific patterns precede generic ones. `is connections list` is a DEFENSIVE fixture proving both GSuite connections serving Shared are valid/Enabled; the bogus connection 00000000-... is intentionally NOT in that list, corroborating that the referenced connection is not resolvable. `or jobs traces` returns the real non-JSON Failure envelope the live CLI produced.

unmocked_default._doc: Any command with no matching rule returns an empty JSON array with exit 0, mirroring an empty list result. Keeps the agent from hard-failing on an unrecorded probe.

expected_calls.description: Agent lists folders to resolve folder Shared to its key (1965a46b-...).
Agent finds the most-recent Faulted job in Shared (4e00b4ca is the latest by StartTime).
Agent reads the faulted job's Info, exposing the GSuiteException / ConnectionHttpException stack and the bogus connection id.
OPTIONAL — Agent may list connections to confirm the bogus connection id (00000000-...) is not present and both real GSuite connections are Enabled. Tracked-but-not-required.
OPTIONAL — traces are unavailable for this job (non-JSON Failure envelope); tracked-but-not-required.
